title: pinus ponderosa subsp. jeffreyi
intro: Pinus ponderosa subsp. jeffreyi, also known as the Jeffrey pine, is a subspecies of the ponderosa pine tree, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The Jeffrey pine has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like pinene and limonene, that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of the Jeffrey pine include reducing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of the Jeffrey pine include essential oils, extracts, and teas, which can be used topically or ingested after proper dilution and consultation with a healthcare professional.
